questions.q: Rajendra Prasad became independent India's first holder of which office?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad became the first President of the Republic of India in 1950.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad joined Gandhi during which early campaign in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad joined Gandhi during the Champaran campaign in Bihar in 1917.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad presided over which body that framed India's Constitution?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad presided over the Constituent Assembly that framed the Constitution of India.

questions.q: In which year did Rajendra Prasad become the first President of the Republic of India?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad became the first President of the Republic of India in 1950, when the Constitution came into force.

questions.q: What was Rajendra Prasad's profession before entering the national movement?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was a successful lawyer before he dedicated himself to the freedom struggle.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad was a leader of which political organization during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was a senior leader of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: From which present-day Indian state did Rajendra Prasad hail?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad came from Bihar, where he led the Champaran campaign with Gandhi.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad worked closely under the leadership of which figure in the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was a devoted follower of Mahatma Gandhi, whom he joined during the Champaran campaign.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad is commonly remembered by which honorific reflecting his stature?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was affectionately called Rajen Babu and honoured with the title Deshratna, 'Jewel of the Country'.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad served as President of India for how many full terms, making him the only person to do so?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad served two terms as President of India, the only person to hold the office twice.

questions.q: In which year did the Champaran Satyagraha, which Rajendra Prasad joined, take place?
questions.explanation: The Champaran Satyagraha, in which Rajendra Prasad assisted Gandhi, took place in 1917.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad was elected president of the Indian National Congress for the first time in which year?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad first became Congress president at the Bombay session in 1934.

questions.q: In independent India's first cabinet of 1946-47, Rajendra Prasad held which portfolio before becoming President?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad served as Minister for Food and Agriculture in the interim and first cabinet before becoming President.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad was elected to preside over the Constituent Assembly in which year?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was elected president of the Constituent Assembly in December 1946 when it first convened.

questions.q: Which devastating natural disaster in 1934 saw Rajendra Prasad lead relief efforts in his home region?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad organised large-scale relief during the catastrophic Bihar earthquake of 1934.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad was awarded India's highest civilian honour. Which one?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was honoured with the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad took part in which mass movement launched by Gandhi in 1930?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad participated in the Civil Disobedience Movement launched with the Salt March in 1930 and was imprisoned.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad's autobiography, written largely in prison, was titled?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad wrote his autobiography, titled 'Atmakatha', much of it during imprisonment.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad later wrote a noted account of his association with Gandhi titled?
options: At the Feet of Mahatma Gandhi
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad authored 'At the Feet of Mahatma Gandhi', recalling his long association with Gandhi.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad presided over the Constituent Assembly when it adopted the Constitution on which date in 1949?
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949, with Rajendra Prasad presiding over the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad demitted the office of President in which year, near the end of his second term?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad stepped down as President in 1962 after serving two terms.

questions.q: In which year did Rajendra Prasad pass away, a year after leaving the presidency?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad died in 1963, the year after he retired from the presidency.

questions.q: Before joining the freedom movement, Rajendra Prasad was an outstanding student. He topped examinations at which prestigious university?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was a brilliant scholar who topped his examinations at the University of Calcutta.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad helped found and support which nationalist educational institution in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad helped establish the Bihar Vidyapeeth, a national educational institution, during the Non-Cooperation era.

questions.q: As Congress president, Rajendra Prasad stepped in to lead the party in 1939 after which leader's resignation?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad became Congress president in 1939 following the resignation of Subhas Chandra Bose.

questions.q: During his presidency, Rajendra Prasad had a notable difference of opinion with the government over which major reform legislation?
questions.explanation: As President, Rajendra Prasad expressed reservations over aspects of the Hindu Code Bill, leading to debate over presidential powers.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad gave up a lucrative legal practice to respond to which of Gandhi's calls in 1920-21?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad abandoned his flourishing law practice to join Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement in the early 1920s.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad was president of the Congress session held at which city in 1934?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad presided over the Bombay session of the Congress in 1934, his first term as president.

questions.q: Rajendra Prasad was first elected President of India in 1950 by which body, before direct election by the electoral college in 1952?
options: The members of the Constituent Assembly
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad was first chosen President in 1950 by the Constituent Assembly, and re-elected in 1952 by the formal electoral college.

questions.q: After retiring from the presidency, Rajendra Prasad spent his final period associated with which institution in Patna?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad spent his last days at the Sadaqat Ashram in Patna, a centre he had long been associated with.
